channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A ‘true crime’ YouTube channel’s videos got millions of views. It turns out the stories were AI-generated
item.description: A popular “true crime” YouTube channel has been pulling in millions of views with videos about gruesome murders. As it turns out, none of them are real.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI turns down Musk's takeover bid
item.description: OpenAI’s board of directors has turned down Elon Musk’s $97.4 billion bid to buy the nonprofit that controls the artificial intelligence company. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netflix releases trailer for new western series Ransom Canyon and it looks like an exciting mix of Yellowstone and Virgin River
item.description: Netflix unveils trailer for new western series Ransom Canyon and it looks perfect for fans of Yellowstone and Virgin River.
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE team may need a crash course in COBOL
item.description: Young software engineers from Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) continue to infiltrate U.S. government agencies with the stated goal of eliminating what the Trump White House deems wasteful (or “woke”) spending.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Furious Garmin users claim the company has 'abandoned' the Fenix 7 range as another update snubs older models
item.description: Garmin Fenix 7 users are worried they're being left behind by the company's chequered update schedule.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Apple Expands Services Beyond Its Ecosystem - A Strategic Pivot
item.description: Apple's move to introduce its Apple TV app on Android marks a significant shift in strategy, expanding services beyond its own ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency hit by another privacy lawsuit with millions impacted
item.description: The Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) joined the wave of lawsuits against the new government agency over supposedly illegal data access.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: New iPhone 17 Pro rumor predicts bizarre new camera bar design, but we're far from convinced
item.description: A rumor suggests the iPhone 17 Pro could have a massive camera bar, and the iPhone 17 Pro Max could have a smaller Dynamic Island.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Buckle up for the supercharged Python interpreter
item.description: In this edition of our biweekly Python newsletter: The next version of Python brings an all-new variety of speed boost. Python packages may soon pack better labeling for their ingredients. Cython 3.1 tees up new features for converting Python to C.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Instagram’s AI bots are often sexually suggestive—and sometimes underage
item.description: When Meta launched its “AI Studio” feature for over two billion Instagram users in July 2024, the company promised a tool that would give anyone the ability to create their own AI characters “to make you laugh, generate memes, give travel advice, and so much more.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: SAP: Latest news and insights
item.description: SAP is an enterprise software vendor based in Walldorf, Germany. Its cloud and on-premises enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, including S/4HANA, helps organizations manage their business operations and customer relations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Innovators gear up work on ‘green’ hydrogen plane with plans for nonstop 9-day trip around Earth
item.description: Swiss aviation pioneer Bertrand Piccard is developing a plane powered by liquid hydrogen after a much-hyped flight in 2015 that used sunlight to travel around the world.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten And Mustakim Hasnath, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Archaeologists unearth the remains of a Roman basilica on the site of a new London skyscraper
item.description: Work to give London yet another skyscraper has uncovered traces of the city’s origins almost 2,000 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI company Ross Intelligence loses copyright fight with Thomson Reuters
item.description: A US judge has ruled in favor of Thomson Reuters in a AI training fight against Ross Intelligence, a legal AI startup, according to The Verge. Thomson Reuters sued Ross Intelligence in 2020 for using the company’s legal research platform Westlaw to train Ross Intelligence’s AI without permission.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Don’t use public ASP.NET keys (duh), Microsoft warns
item.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence in December observed a “threat actor” using a publicly available ASP.NET machine key to inject malicious code and fetch the Godzilla post-exploitation framework, a “backdoor” web shell used by intruders to execute commands and manipulate files.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Don't fall for that sneaky iCloud storage alert in your inbox
item.description: Beware of iCloud scams using urgent messages. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you spot the red flags to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The cloud giants stumble
item.description: Recent financial results from the major cloud providers reveal a concerning trend in cloud adoption and growth. AWS reported just 13% growth in the final quarter of 2023, Microsoft Azure saw 19% growth, and Google Cloud achieved 26% growth—all below market expectations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: VPN vs. ZTNA: Cisco tackles pros and cons
item.description: It’s no secret that more modern approaches to remote access have been usurping VPNs as organizations adapt to the realities of a distributed workforce, cloud-based applications, and heightened security threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4
